name: aiolifx-themes

metadata.name: aiolifx-themes
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Async library that applies color themes to LIFX lights

documentation:
# aiolifx-themes

Async library that applies color themes to LIFX lights ## Installation Install this via pip (or your favourite package manager): `pip install aiolifx-themes` ## Contributors ✨ Thanks goes to these wonderful people ([emoji key](https://allcontributors.org/docs/en/emoji-key)):

Stephen Moore
💻
Avi Miller
💻 📖 🚧
This project follows the [all-contributors](https://github.com/all-contributors/all-contributors) specification. Contributions of any kind welcome! ## Credits This package contains code originally authored by [@delfick](https://github.com/delfick) for [Photons](https://photons.delfick.com). This package was created with [Cookiecutter](https://github.com/audreyr/cookiecutter) and the [browniebroke/cookiecutter-pypackage](https://github.com/browniebroke/cookiecutter-pypackage) project template.

documentation: Utility methods for aiolifx-themes.

functions.name: aiolifx_themes.util.is_single
documentation: True if device is a single zone light.

documentation: True if device is a linear multizone light.

functions.name: aiolifx_themes.util.is_matrix
documentation: True is device is a matrix light.

functions.name: aiolifx_themes.util.supports_extended_multizone
documentation: True if device is an extended multizone light.

functions.name: aiolifx_themes.util.user_coords_to_pixel_coords
documentation:
Translation between user_coords_and_sizes to based on pixels.

The user coords are what is stored on the tiles themselves
`[(user_x, user_y), (width, height)]`
where user_x and user_y are the center of the tile in terms of tile
and width and height are in terms of pixels.

We return from this function
`[(user_x, user_y), (width, height)]`
where width and height are unchanged
and user_x and user_y are the top left of the tile in terms of pixels

documentation: A color is less than another color if it has lower HSBK values.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.theme.ThemeColor.__gt__
documentation: A color is more than another color if it has higher HSBK values.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.theme.ThemeColor.__eq__
documentation: Two colors are equal if they have the same HSBK values.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.theme.ThemeColor.__hash__

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.theme.ThemeColor.as_dict
documentation: Returns a dictionary of hue, saturation, brightness and kelvin.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.theme.ThemeColor.as_tuple
documentation:
Returns a tuple of 16 bit hue, saturation, brightness and kelvin values.

The hue, saturation and brightness values are converted to 16 bit values
each with a range of 0-65535.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.theme.ThemeColor.cache_key
documentation: Returns a tuple of tuples for hue, saturation, brightness and kelvin.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.theme.ThemeColor.clone
documentation: Return another color with the same HSBK values.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.theme.ThemeColor.limit_distance_to
documentation:
Return a new color with its hue within 90 degrees of this color.

This adds or subtracts 90 degrees from the hue value based on whether
the other color is more or less than 180 degrees from this one.

If the difference between the two colors is less than 90 degrees, we
return the original color.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.theme.ThemeColor.__str__
documentation: Return a string representation of the HSBK values for this color.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.theme.ThemeColor.__repr__
documentation: Return a string representation of the HSBK values for this color.
Represents a color that can be contained in a theme.

modules.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas
documentation: The Canvas is a class for storing a two-dimensional grid of colors for matrix devices.

functions.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.color_weighting
documentation: Return an array of colors where there is more of a color the closer it is.

functions.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.shuffle_point
documentation: Return a new (i, j) value that is the current (i, j) value plus or minus a random amount.

functions.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.surrounding_points
documentation: Return the points that surround the specified point.

classes.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.__init__
documentation: Initialize the canvas.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.add_points_for_tile
documentation:
Create points on the canvas around where a tile is.

We create an area that's half the tile width/height beyond the boundary
of the tile.

We also spread the points out in a random manner and try to avoid having
points next to each other.

Multiple calls to this function will not override existing points on the
canvas

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.surrounding_colors
documentation:
Return the colors that surround this (i, j) point.

This will only return points that exist.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.has_neighbour
documentation: Return whether there are any points around this (i, j) position

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.shuffle_points
documentation: Take all the points and move them around a random amount

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.blur
documentation:
For each point, find the average colour of that point plus all surrounding
points.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.blur_by_distance
documentation:
Similar to blur but will find the 8 closest points as opposed to the 8
surrounding points.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.points_for_tile
documentation:
Return a list of 64 hsbk values for this tile

For any point on the tile that doesn't have a corresponding point on the
canvas return a grey value. This is useful for when we tell the applier
to not fill in the gaps.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.fill_in_points
documentation:
Fill in the gaps on this canvas by blurring the points on the provided
canvas around where our tile is.

We blur by finding the 4 closest points for each point on our tile and
averaging them.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.closest_points
documentation: Return `[(distance, color), ...]` for the closest consider amount of points to (i, j)

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.__iter__
documentation: Yield `((i, j), color)` pairs for all our points

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.get
documentation: Get a point or the passed in default_color value if the point doesn't exist

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.__getitem__
documentation: Return the color at `point` where `point` is `(i, j)`

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.__setitem__
documentation: Set the color at `point` where `point` is `(i, j)`

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.canvas.Canvas.__contains__
documentation: Return whether this `point` has a color where `point` is `(i, j)`

A Canvas is a collection of points with methods for interacting with those points.

The points are stored as (i, j) in a dictionary. The value for each point is a ThemeColor object.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.generators.matrix.MatrixGenerator.add_tiles_from_canvas
documentation: Add HSBK values to our colors given this canvas and our coordinates.

methods.name: aiolifx_themes.generators.matrix.MatrixGenerator.get_theme_colors
documentation:
This method creates a new canvas with random points around where each tile is,
then shuffles those points and blurs them a little. Next, it fills the gaps between
the points and blurs the fill as well.

The mirrors the output you get when applying a theme in the LIFX smart phone app.

The output is a list of 1 to 5 lists of 64 HSBK values, representing 64 zones per tile
with each list corresponding to a tile in the device chain.

Used to generate a random distribution of colors on a two-dimensional matrix device.

coords_and_sizes = [((t.user_x, t.user_y), (t.width, t.height)) for t in chain]

> applier = TileApplier.from_user_coords(coords_and_sizes)
>
> for i, colors in enumerate(applier.apply_theme(theme)):
>
> : \# Apply colors to tile index i

coords_and_sizes

: A list of `((left_x, top_y), (width, height))` representing the top
  left corner of each tile in the chain.

  Note that if you have `((user_x, user_y), (width, height))` values from
  asking a tile for its device chain, then use the `from_user_coords`
  classmethod to create a TileApplier from that data.

just_points

: Part of the algorithm generates random points on the canvas followed by
  filling in the gaps between those points. By setting this option to True
  we won't fill in the gaps

post_blur

: By setting this to false we won't blur the points after the gaps are
  filled in.
